title: Manage static leakage with cell choice and gating
description: Use high-threshold cells on non-critical paths and power-gate idle regions to cut leakage current.
body: Use when static power matters on a design that idles in low-activity states. Do not use low-threshold fast cells everywhere, since they leak heavily even when idle. Actions: assign high-threshold cells to paths with timing slack, power-gate regions that sit idle, and verify leakage in power analysis. Validation signals include reduced static power in the report and timing still met after the cell swaps. Risk boundary: over-using slow cells can break timing on critical paths, so escalate to a mixed-threshold strategy when slack disappears.
